# Amal Foundation

Amal Foundation is a private non-profit foundation established in 2025 as the dedicated charitable arm of Amal Group International Limited.

## Mission
To foster community development and poverty reduction through highly targeted, sustainable programs across Somalia and the broader region.

## Vision
A world where every Somali community has access to education, healthcare, economic dignity, and food security.

## Parent Organisation
Amal Group International Limited — a diversified group operating since 1997 across financial services (Amal Bank, Amal Express), telecommunications (Amtel), trade (Amal Trade), and real estate (Amal Real Estate & Construction).

## Programmes & Initiatives
- Outreach & Networking: strategic alliances with foundations, NGOs, and global donors to multiply reach and credibility
- Technology for Community Development (Amal Hub): technology skills, freelancing, and innovation platform for Somali youth — remote-first and diaspora-powered
- Women Economic Empowerment & Financial Inclusion: Sharia-compliant finance through Amal Bank, business education, and peer support circles for women and youth
- Sustainable Agriculture & Food Security: solar-powered irrigation, climate-resilient crops, and food sovereignty for smallholder farmers

## Areas Served
- Garowe, Puntland, Somalia (headquarters)
- Mogadishu, Somalia
- Nairobi, Kenya (programme delivery hub)
- Somali diaspora communities in Europe, North America, and the Gulf

## Key Facts
- Founded: 2025 by board resolution of Amal Group International Limited
- Incorporated as a private non-profit foundation in Somalia
- Headquarters: Garowe Main Road, Garowe, Puntland, Somalia
- Primary beneficiaries: women, single mothers, youth, smallholder farmers, and underserved communities across Somalia
- Backed by: Amal Group International Limited — parent of Amal Bank, Amal Express, Amtel, Amal Trade, and Amal Real Estate & Construction
